steps.instructions: Remove the Medfusion 4000 from patient use if the Motor Rate Error occurs during an active infusion. Transfer the infusion to another verified pump if therapy must continue.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the display shows Motor Rate Error. Note whether it occurs during startup, syringe loading, after pressing start, during infusion, or intermittently.

steps.title: Remove the Syringe and Check for Obvious Mechanical Resistance
steps.instructions: Remove the syringe and inspect the syringe loading area, plunger driver, syringe clamp area, and flange area for obstruction, sticky residue, dried medication, cracked plastic, or anything interfering with movement.

steps.title: Inspect the Syringe and Accessory Setup
steps.instructions: Confirm the syringe type and size are appropriate. Check for damaged syringe parts, sticky plunger movement, angled loading, or setup issues. Test with a known-good compatible syringe if available.

steps.title: Check the Plunger Driver Movement
steps.instructions: With the pump removed from patient use, observe plunger driver movement during controlled testing or diagnostic operation. Listen for grinding, clicking, slipping, binding, or uneven motor movement.

steps.title: Inspect for Physical Damage or Drop Evidence
steps.instructions: Check the housing, plunger driver, syringe clamp, and drive area for cracks, looseness, impact damage, or misalignment. Ask whether the pump was dropped or struck.

steps.title: Confirm Power and Battery Condition
steps.instructions: Connect the pump to a known-good AC power source and confirm stable power with no low battery, charging, or power-related warnings.

steps.title: Run Basic Functional Verification
steps.instructions: Run the pump under controlled bench conditions using an appropriate test syringe and standard test setup. Observe whether the Motor Rate Error returns.

steps.title: Evaluate Whether the Issue Is Accessory, Setup, or Device Related
steps.instructions: If the alarm only occurs with one syringe or setup, suspect syringe compatibility, syringe damage, or loading technique. If it occurs with multiple known-good syringes, suspect pump drive or sensing failure.

steps.title: Do Not Continue Clinical Use if the Alarm Recurs
steps.instructions: If Motor Rate Error returns after external checks, remove the pump from service and send it for repair evaluation.

CCR.Complaint: Smiths Medical Medfusion 4000 reported Motor Rate Error during infusion setup/use. Pump alarmed and could not be trusted to run at the programmed rate.
CCR.Cause: Checked external setup, syringe loading, power source, and plunger drive area. No syringe obstruction or accessory issue found. Alarm recurred with known-good syringe, indicating likely internal motor sensing or drive-train failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed pump from patient use, labeled Out of Service, and sent for repair evaluation of motor assembly, worm gear, worm coupling, motor bracket, and related drive-train components.

title: Smiths Medical Medfusion 4000 Infusion Pump - Alarm: Calculated Rate Out Of Range
description: Troubleshooting Calculated Rate Out Of Range alarms caused by syringe size, concentration, dose, volume, rate, or drug library limits that prevent the Medfusion 4000 from accepting the programmed infusion.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Notify clinical staff immediately if the alarm prevents infusion from starting or continuing. Do not make repeated programming changes while the patient depends on the pump. Use a verified backup pump or approved backup method if therapy is time-critical.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the display shows Calculated Rate Out Of Range and identify whether it occurs during programming, after drug program selection, after entering dose or concentration values, when attempting to start, or only with a specific syringe or drug library entry.

steps.title: Confirm the Syringe Size and Syringe Selection
steps.instructions: Check the physical syringe and confirm the programmed syringe size and manufacturer match. Reload the syringe if detection appears incorrect.

steps.title: Review the Ordered Infusion Parameters
steps.instructions: Have clinical staff verify drug name, dose, concentration, volume to be infused, infusion time, patient weight if applicable, and dosing units.

steps.title: Re-Enter the Programming Values
steps.instructions: Cancel the programming attempt if safe and re-enter all values carefully, checking decimal placement, units, concentration, patient weight, volume, time, and bolus settings.

steps.title: Check Whether the Rate Is Truly Outside the Pump or Program Range
steps.instructions: Review the calculated rate and compare it against syringe, pump, drug program, and facility configuration limits.

steps.title: Try a Clinically Appropriate Alternate Syringe Size
steps.instructions: If approved by clinical staff and pharmacy policy, test whether the intended therapy can be programmed with another appropriate syringe size. Do not use syringe size as an unsafe workaround.

steps.title: Verify the Selected Drug Program
steps.instructions: Confirm the correct drug program, care area, concentration profile, and dosing mode were selected.

steps.title: Check for Configuration or Drug Library Limit Conflict
steps.instructions: If the alarm follows a specific drug program, care area, concentration, or dosing mode, document the combination and escalate to the PharmGuard Toolbox administrator or drug library owner.

steps.title: Test With a Basic Non-Patient Setup, If Allowed
steps.instructions: Using a non-patient setup and facility-approved process, attempt a basic infusion setup outside the affected drug program to determine whether normal programming is accepted.

steps.title: Determine Whether the Issue Is Pump-Specific or Configuration-Wide
steps.instructions: Compare behavior with another Medfusion 4000 using the same drug program and values. Do not connect either pump to a patient during comparison.

CCR.Complaint: Medfusion 4000 reported Calculated Rate Out Of Range alarm during programming. User stated pump would not accept infusion parameters for selected drug program and syringe.
CCR.Cause: Syringe size, concentration, dose/rate, volume, and infusion time were reviewed. Alarm was associated with calculated rate exceeding the allowable range for the selected drug program/configuration.
CCR.Resolution: Re-entered programming values and verified syringe selection. Alarm persisted only under the selected drug program. Pump removed from patient use pending PharmGuard Toolbox configuration review by drug library administrator.

title: Smiths Medical Medfusion 4000 Infusion Pump - Alarm: Force Sensor BGND Test
description: Troubleshooting Force Sensor BGND Test alarms affecting plunger force sensing, occlusion detection, and pump trustworthiness during startup.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the pump from patient use immediately if it is connected to a patient or prepared for active infusion. Use another verified infusion pump if therapy must continue.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the pump on and confirm the exact alarm displayed is Force Sensor BGND Test. Document whether the alarm occurs during startup, immediately after POST, intermittently, after handling, after cleaning, after impact, or after syringe loading or plunger driver movement.

steps.title: Power the Pump Off as Directed
steps.instructions: Turn the pump off according to the displayed instruction, wait briefly, then power it back on without touching or moving the plunger driver. Keep the pump stable during startup.

steps.title: Check for Plunger Driver Movement During Startup
steps.instructions: Ask staff whether the pump was handled, cleaned, bumped, or loaded when the alarm occurred. Inspect the plunger driver area for anything pressing on or restricting movement.

steps.title: Inspect the Syringe and Plunger Area
steps.instructions: Remove any syringe from the pump. Check the syringe holder, plunger driver, plunger head, and surrounding area for damage, residue, cracked plastic, impact signs, fluid contamination, obstruction, stiffness, or binding.

steps.title: Check External Condition and Recent Handling
steps.instructions: Inspect the case, handle, display area, syringe channel, and plunger head for visible damage. Ask whether the alarm began after a fall, impact, transport, or cleaning event.

steps.title: Verify Power Source and Startup Stability
steps.instructions: Connect the pump to a known-good AC outlet using the correct power cord. Confirm the pump powers on normally and does not reset, flicker, or lose power during startup.

steps.title: Check the Force Sensor Reading in Biomed Diagnostics
steps.instructions: Access Biomed > Diagnostics > Monitor Analog Sensors. Monitor the plunger force sensor reading and confirm it is stable at rest and responds predictably during appropriate bench testing.

steps.title: Recalibrate the Force Sensor if Startup Movement Was Suspected
steps.instructions: If the failure appears related to plunger driver movement, handling, or bumping during startup, perform force sensor calibration using the proper Biomed calibration process. Power cycle the pump and repeat startup verification.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: If the alarm clears after stable startup, obstruction removal, or force sensor recalibration, verify normal startup and appropriate force sensor behavior. Return to service only after required safety, functional, and operational checks are completed.

CCR.Complaint: Smiths Medical Medfusion 4000 reported Force Sensor BGND Test alarm during startup. Pump failed force sensor self-test and was removed from patient use for evaluation.
CCR.Cause: Force sensor background test failure confirmed. External obstruction, syringe loading issue, and startup handling were checked. Alarm may be related to plunger driver movement during startup, force sensor calibration, plunger cable, force sensor cable, Plunger PCB, or force sensor failure.
CCR.Resolution: Pump was powered off and restarted without plunger movement. Plunger area inspected for obstruction, damage, contamination, or impact. Force sensor checked in Biomed > Diagnostics > Monitor Analog Sensors. Force sensor recalibration performed if appropriate. If alarm persisted, pump was labeled Out of Service and routed for repair of the plunger force sensing system.

title: Smiths Medical Medfusion 4000 Infusion Pump - Alarm: Depleted Battery
description: Troubleshooting Depleted Battery alarm conditions that may prevent the Smiths Medical Medfusion 4000 infusion pump from safely operating on battery power.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the Medfusion 4000 is actively infusing and a Depleted Battery alarm is present, notify clinical staff immediately. Connect the pump to AC power or have clinical staff transfer the infusion to another verified pump if reliable power cannot be restored.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the exact alarm shown on the pump display is Depleted Battery. Observe whether the issue occurs at startup, during infusion, when unplugged from AC, after charging, or intermittently.

steps.title: Remove the Pump From Battery-Dependent Use
steps.instructions: Do not return the pump to normal clinical use until battery operation is verified. Keep the pump connected to AC power during evaluation and label it Out of Service if it cannot remain powered reliably.

steps.title: Verify the AC Power Source
steps.instructions: Plug the pump into a known-good hospital-grade outlet. Confirm the outlet is live and not controlled by a wall switch, power strip, or failed emergency power circuit.

steps.title: Check the AC Power Cord and Connection
steps.instructions: Inspect the power cord for damage, looseness, bent prongs, cuts, strain, or missing ground pin. Confirm the cord is fully seated into the pump and wall outlet, and test with a known-good compatible cord if available.

steps.title: Verify AC and Battery Indicators
steps.instructions: With the pump connected to AC power, check the AC power indicator and battery or charging indicator. Confirm whether the pump shows AC power connected, battery charging, battery not charging, battery depleted, battery fault, or abnormal battery status.

steps.title: Allow Charging Time and Recheck
steps.instructions: If the pump had been stored unplugged or used on battery for an extended period, leave it connected to AC power long enough for charging to begin. Recheck the display and battery indicator after charging time has passed.

steps.title: Check Battery Status in Biomed Diagnostics
steps.instructions: Access Biomed > Diagnostics > Monitor Battery Status. Review battery capacity, charge state, charge cycles, and any abnormal battery information available.

steps.title: Check for Charge-Cycle Limit
steps.instructions: Review the battery charge-cycle count in the battery status screen if available. Replace the battery pack according to facility procedure if the count has reached the manufacturer’s stated limit of 400 cycles.

steps.title: Evaluate Battery Capacity
steps.instructions: Review the reported full-capacity value. If the full-capacity value has dropped below the manufacturer’s specified threshold, replace the battery pack.

steps.title: Replace the Battery Pack if Indicated
steps.instructions: If battery capacity is low, charge-cycle limit is reached, or the battery will not recover after charging, replace the battery pack. Connect the pump to AC power and verify the new battery is recognized.

steps.title: Verify Battery Operation Before Return to Service
steps.instructions: After charging or replacing the battery, confirm the pump operates properly on AC power. Briefly verify that the pump remains powered when disconnected from AC according to facility policy, and confirm that no battery alarm returns.

CCR.Complaint: Medfusion 4000 infusion pump reported with Depleted Battery alarm. Staff stated pump would not reliably support operation on battery power and alarm appeared when disconnected from AC.
CCR.Cause: Battery status checked in Biomed diagnostics. Battery capacity was below acceptable level and/or charge-cycle count reached replacement limit. AC outlet and power cord were verified good.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced battery pack, connected pump to AC power, verified charging indication and battery status, confirmed alarm cleared, and performed functional power checks. Pump returned to service after passing verification.

title: Smiths Medical Medfusion 4000 Infusion Pump - Alarm: Calibration Required
description: Troubleshooting Calibration Required alarms on the Medfusion 4000 that indicate the pump should be removed from service until calibration, maintenance, or verification checks are completed.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the Medfusion 4000 is connected to a patient and a Calibration Required alarm appears, do not continue relying on the pump for medication or fluid delivery. Transfer the infusion to another verified pump according to clinical procedure.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error/Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the exact alarm displayed is Calibration Required. Note whether it appears at startup, during programming, when loading a syringe, during infusion, after damage or repair, or during maintenance review.

steps.title: Remove the Pump From Clinical Use
steps.instructions: Remove the pump from patient care areas, attach an Out of Service label, and prevent the pump from being placed back into circulation until calibration or verification is completed.

steps.title: Check for Recent Damage or Drop History
steps.instructions: Inspect for cracked housing, loose syringe clamp, damaged plunger driver, misaligned syringe loading area, contamination, damaged connectors, or evidence of a drop.

steps.title: Verify Power and Startup Behavior
steps.instructions: Connect the pump to a known-good AC power source, power it on, and check whether it starts normally aside from the Calibration Required alarm.

steps.title: Check External Condition Before Calibration
steps.instructions: Inspect the syringe loading area, syringe clamp, plunger driver, barrel area, battery door, and external connections. Clean external contamination using facility-approved procedures.

steps.title: Confirm Whether Annual Maintenance Is Due
steps.instructions: Review CMMS history, maintenance records, or calibration stickers to determine whether annual maintenance or calibration verification is due or overdue.

steps.title: Perform Required Calibration or Calibration Verification
steps.instructions: Use the appropriate Clinical Engineering test setup and Biomed Calibration menu to complete required calibration or verification for syringe size, position, force sensor, plunger position, and related functions.

steps.title: Perform Functional Checks After Calibration
steps.instructions: Confirm the pump powers on without the alarm, syringe detection works correctly, syringe loading is normal, programming is successful, force or occlusion checks pass if required, and no additional alarms appear.

steps.title: Return the Pump to Service Only After Passing Checks
steps.instructions: After the pump passes calibration, maintenance, and functional checks, update the maintenance sticker or CMMS status, remove the Out of Service label, and return the pump to clinical service.

CCR.Complaint: Medfusion 4000 reported Calibration Required alarm at startup. Pump was removed from clinical use because calibration status could affect normal infusion reliability.
CCR.Cause: Annual calibration verification was due. No external damage or syringe loading obstruction found during inspection.
CCR.Resolution: Removed pump from service, performed required annual maintenance and calibration verification through Biomed Calibration functions. Pump passed post-calibration functional checks and was returned to service.

title: Smiths Medical Medfusion 4000 Infusion Pump - Alarm: Configuration Required
description: Troubleshooting a Configuration Required alarm on the Smiths Medical Medfusion 4000 when the pump lacks a valid setup and cannot be safely used for infusion.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the pump from patient use immediately if the alarm appears during setup or before an infusion. Do not bypass the alarm or use the pump for medication delivery until the correct configuration is installed and verified.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power on the pump and confirm the displayed alarm is Configuration Required. Document whether it appears at startup, after service, after a configuration update, after network activity, or during care area or drug selection.

steps.title: Confirm the Pump Is Not Being Used Clinically
steps.instructions: Check whether the pump was assigned to a patient care area or staged for use. Notify clinical staff that the pump should not be used until Clinical Engineering verifies the configuration.

steps.title: Check for Simple Setup or Selection Issues
steps.instructions: Review whether the pump is asking for a profile, care area, drug library, or configuration selection. Confirm the user did not select an unavailable or incorrect program option.

steps.title: Verify Power and Battery Stability
steps.instructions: Connect the pump to a known-good AC power source. Confirm the pump remains powered without restarting, shutting down, or losing settings during startup.

steps.title: Check for Recent Service, Update, or Configuration Activity
steps.instructions: Determine whether the pump was recently repaired, updated, reconfigured, moved between departments, added to a new drug library group, connected to PharmGuard Toolbox, cleared, reset, or restored.

steps.title: Confirm the Correct Facility Configuration Is Available
steps.instructions: Use the appropriate facility workflow to verify the approved Medfusion 4000 configuration and drug program exist in PharmGuard Toolbox or the approved configuration management system.

steps.title: Reinstall or Teach the Approved Configuration
steps.instructions: Use the approved PharmGuard Toolbox or network configuration workflow to load the correct validated configuration to the pump. Follow facility procedure for teaching the configuration to the pump.

steps.title: Verify Configuration After Loading
steps.instructions: Restart the pump and confirm the alarm does not return. Verify expected care areas, drug programs, infusion modes, limits, and facility settings are present.

steps.title: Perform Functional Readiness Checks
steps.instructions: Confirm display and keypad response, startup behavior, alarm tone operation, AC and battery status, syringe detection/setup behavior, expected configuration access, and no recurring configuration alarm after reboot.

CCR.Complaint: Smiths Medical Medfusion 4000 displayed Configuration Required alarm at startup. Pump was not available for clinical use due to missing or invalid configuration/drug program setup.
CCR.Cause: Pump did not have a valid facility-approved configuration loaded, or configuration was incomplete/invalid following setup or service activity.
CCR.Resolution: Removed pump from clinical use. Verified approved facility configuration in PharmGuard Toolbox/network workflow. Loaded and taught correct configuration to pump. Restarted pump, confirmed Configuration Required alarm cleared, verified expected care areas/drug programs, and returned pump to service.

title: Smiths Medical Medfusion 4000 Infusion Pump - Alarm: Force Sensor Bridge Test
description: Troubleshooting Force Sensor Bridge Test alarm affecting force sensing and occlusion detection reliability on the Smiths Medical Medfusion 4000 infusion pump.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the Medfusion 4000 from patient use immediately if the alarm is active or recurring. Use another verified infusion pump for patient therapy.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the pump on in a controlled Biomed setting and confirm the exact alarm shown is Force Sensor Bridge Test. Note when the alarm occurs and document related symptoms.

steps.title: Remove Obvious External Causes First
steps.instructions: Inspect the syringe loading area, plunger drive area, and force sensing contact area for contamination, fluid residue, cracked plastic, or physical obstruction. Clean external surfaces according to facility-approved procedures.

steps.title: Check for Physical Damage
steps.instructions: Inspect the pump for drop damage, cracked housing, loose plunger assembly, misaligned syringe loading components, sticky plunger movement, fluid intrusion, or damaged connectors.

steps.title: Access Biomed Diagnostics
steps.instructions: Use Biomed > Diagnostics > Monitor Analog Sensors to observe the force sensor reading and determine whether it is stable, responsive, reasonable, and free from excessive drift or noise.

steps.title: Evaluate Force Sensor Behavior
steps.instructions: With the pump removed from patient use, gently observe force sensor response during appropriate diagnostic testing. Do not use excessive force.

steps.title: Recalibrate the Force Sensor if Appropriate
steps.instructions: If facility policy allows Clinical Engineering calibration and the pump is physically intact, perform approved force sensor calibration and repeat diagnostic checks.

steps.title: Confirm Alarm Resolution
steps.instructions: Power cycle the pump and confirm the alarm does not return. Verify startup, force sensor stability, occlusion detection-related checks, and required operational testing before return to service.

CCR.Complaint: Medfusion 4000 infusion pump reported Force Sensor Bridge Test alarm during startup. Pump could not be cleared for patient use due to force sensing system failure affecting occlusion detection reliability.
CCR.Cause: Force sensor diagnostic check showed abnormal force sensor behavior consistent with force sensing circuit failure. External syringe area and plunger path inspected with no user-correctable obstruction found.
CCR.Resolution: Removed pump from service. Checked force sensor behavior in Biomed > Diagnostics > Monitor Analog Sensors. Force sensor alarm persisted after evaluation. Labeled device Out of Service and sent for repair or bench evaluation of plunger force sensor, force sensor cable, Plunger PCB, and Main PCB path.

title: Smiths Medical Medfusion 4000 Infusion Pump - Alarm: Force Sensor Test
description: Troubleshooting Force Sensor Test alarm affecting force sensing verification, occlusion detection confidence, and safe syringe infusion operation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the Medfusion 4000 is connected to a patient and the Force Sensor Test alarm is active, remove the pump from patient use immediately. Transfer the infusion to another verified pump according to clinical procedure.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the exact alarm shown is Force Sensor Test. Document whether it occurs during startup, self-test, syringe loading, diagnostics, or operation.

steps.title: Remove the Pump From Clinical Use
steps.instructions: Do not return the pump to service based only on clearing or acknowledging the alarm. Label the pump Out of Service and move it to a Clinical Engineering bench evaluation area.

steps.title: Check for Obvious External or Mechanical Causes
steps.instructions: Inspect the syringe plunger area, syringe clamp area, and plunger drive area for visible damage, contamination, sticky residue, loose parts, broken plastic, or fluid intrusion.

steps.title: Confirm Syringe Loading and Accessory Conditions
steps.instructions: Install a known-compatible syringe correctly and confirm the barrel, flange, and plunger are seated properly. Rule out syringe loading or accessory-related causes.

steps.title: Check Power and Startup Behavior
steps.instructions: Connect the pump to known-good AC power, power cycle the pump, and observe whether the Force Sensor Test alarm returns during startup or self-test.

steps.title: Use Biomed Diagnostics
steps.instructions: Access the appropriate Biomed or service diagnostic mode according to facility procedure and review force sensor readings for missing, unstable, stuck, or out-of-range behavior.

steps.title: Recalibrate the Force Sensor if Allowed
steps.instructions: If authorized by facility procedure, perform force sensor calibration using the proper calibration method and required test equipment. Do not bypass or force a passing result.

steps.title: Perform Final Verification Before Return to Service
steps.instructions: Verify the Force Sensor Test passes, no related system failure returns, syringe loading works normally, infusion can start without force-related errors, and required operational and safety checks pass.

CCR.Complaint: Medfusion 4000 infusion pump reported Force Sensor Test alarm during startup and could not be cleared for clinical use.
CCR.Cause: Force Sensor Test alarm was confirmed during evaluation. Syringe loading, plunger area condition, and external obstruction checks did not resolve the failure. Force readings required Biomed diagnostic review and calibration evaluation.
CCR.Resolution: Removed pump from service and labeled Out of Service. Checked external plunger area, verified alarm behavior, reviewed force sensing in Biomed diagnostics, and escalated for calibration or repair evaluation.

title: Smiths Medical Medfusion 4000 Infusion Pump - Alarm: Plunger Sensor Failure
description: Troubleshooting guide for a Medfusion 4000 Plunger Sensor Failure alarm that may prevent the pump from verifying syringe plunger engagement and safe infusion readiness.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the pump from patient use immediately if connected to a patient. Transfer the infusion to another verified pump according to clinical procedure before troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the exact alarm shown is Plunger Sensor Failure. Note whether it occurs during startup, syringe loading, infusion start, plunger holder movement, or intermittently during use.

steps.title: Remove the Syringe and Inspect the Plunger Area
steps.instructions: Remove the syringe and inspect the plunger holder, plunger driver, and surrounding area for damage, contamination, debris, fluid residue, medication buildup, or obvious misalignment.

steps.title: Check Plunger Holder Movement
steps.instructions: Gently move the plunger holder through its normal travel range and confirm smooth movement without binding, sticking, excessive looseness, or visible damage.

steps.title: Inspect the Plunger Flippers
steps.instructions: Verify the plunger flippers move freely, return correctly, are not cracked or missing, are not stuck, and are not blocked by contamination.

steps.title: Check for Syringe-Related Causes
steps.instructions: Install a known-good compatible syringe and confirm the syringe is seated correctly with the plunger fully captured by the plunger driver.

steps.title: Run Digital Sensor Diagnostics
steps.instructions: Use Biomed > Diagnostics > Monitor Digital Sensors according to facility procedure and observe plunger loaded sensor states while moving the plunger holder and flippers.

steps.title: Compare Mechanical Movement to Sensor Response
steps.instructions: Confirm that digital sensor state changes match physical flipper movement. Look for stuck, intermittent, late, or mismatched sensor response.

steps.title: Inspect Visible Alignment and Obstruction
steps.instructions: Check the visible plunger flipper and timing area for obvious damage, displacement, contamination, or obstruction. Do not perform deep disassembly during front-line troubleshooting.

steps.title: Perform Functional Verification
steps.instructions: Reload a known-good syringe and verify proper syringe loading, plunger capture, alarm clearance, correct digital sensor response, and no new alarms.

CCR.Complaint: Medfusion 4000 reported Plunger Sensor Failure during syringe loading and would not reliably confirm syringe plunger engagement.
CCR.Cause: Inspected syringe seating, plunger holder movement, and plunger flippers. Digital sensor diagnostics showed abnormal plunger loaded sensor response, indicating a likely plunger sensor, Plunger PCB, or plunger mechanism fault.
CCR.Resolution: Removed pump from patient use, labeled Out of Service, and sent for repair/bench evaluation of the plunger loaded sensor system, flippers, timing components, and plunger mechanism.

title: Smiths Medical Medfusion 4000 Infusion Pump - Alarm: Syringe Flange Sensor Failure
description: Troubleshooting Syringe Flange Sensor Failure when the pump cannot confirm proper syringe flange or ear positioning, which may prevent safe infusion setup and syringe detection.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the Medfusion 4000 from patient use immediately if the alarm appears during clinical use. Transfer the infusion to another verified pump according to clinical procedure.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the exact alarm displayed is Syringe Flange Sensor Failure. Note whether it occurs during startup, syringe loading, flange insertion, ear clip movement, handling, or transport.

steps.title: Remove the Pump From Active Clinical Use
steps.instructions: Do not continue repeated testing while the pump is assigned for patient care. Control the device for Clinical Engineering evaluation if the alarm persists.

steps.title: Inspect the Syringe Flange Holder Area
steps.instructions: Inspect the flange holder for damage, loose parts, residue, debris, impact damage, or misalignment. Clean visible contamination using approved methods.

steps.title: Check Syringe Ear Clip Movement
steps.instructions: Confirm the ear clip moves freely, returns properly, does not stick, and properly captures the syringe flange ears. Do not force the mechanism.

steps.title: Check for Obstruction in the Sensor Path
steps.instructions: Inspect the optical sensor area for residue, lint, broken plastic, label fragments, or fluid contamination that could block the infrared optical interrupter.

steps.title: Test With a Known-Good Syringe
steps.instructions: Load a compatible, known-good syringe of the correct type and size. Confirm the syringe flange ears sit fully in the flange holder.

steps.title: Check for Mechanical Misalignment
steps.instructions: Inspect whether the flange holder and ear clip line up correctly and look for signs of drop damage or shifted syringe loading components.

steps.title: Use Biomed Diagnostics to Confirm Sensor Operation
steps.instructions: Use Biomed > Diagnostics > Monitor Digital Sensors and confirm the flange sensor state changes consistently when the sensor path is blocked and unblocked.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Pattern
steps.instructions: Use diagnostic results to determine whether the issue is related to loading, mechanical alignment, contamination, sensor failure, wiring, or related electronics.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: If the alarm clears after cleaning or correcting the flange holder, ear clip, syringe fit, or obstruction, perform required operational checks before returning the pump to service.

CCR.Complaint: Medfusion 4000 reported Syringe Flange Sensor Failure alarm and would not reliably confirm syringe flange or ear placement in the flange holder.
CCR.Cause: Flange holder, ear clip movement, sensor path, and syringe loading were checked. Flange sensor response in Biomed digital sensor diagnostics was inconsistent, indicating likely flange sensor, ear clip assembly, wiring, or related electronics failure.
CCR.Resolution: Removed pump from service and labeled Out of Service. Sent for repair or bench evaluation of flange sensor, ear clip assembly, wiring, and related electronics. Pump not returned to clinical use.

title: Smiths Medical Medfusion 4000 Infusion Pump - Alarm: Primary Audible Alarm POST
description: Troubleshooting guide for a Medfusion 4000 Primary Audible Alarm POST failure that may prevent normal audible alarm or alert tones from sounding during clinical use.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the pump from patient use, transfer therapy to a verified backup pump if needed, and do not use the device clinically until audible alarm function is verified.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the pump on, confirm the Primary Audible Alarm POST failure, note when it occurs, and listen for audible tones during startup or alarm testing.

steps.title: Confirm the Pump Is Not Muted or Misinterpreted
steps.instructions: Check display, visual indicators, keypad response, and staff-reported symptoms to confirm the issue is related to audible alarm output.

steps.title: Check External Power and Startup Conditions
steps.instructions: Use a known-good AC outlet, confirm the AC indicator, verify normal power-up behavior, and compare battery startup behavior if appropriate.

steps.title: Inspect the Pump Exterior
steps.instructions: Check the housing and speaker openings for damage, liquid intrusion, tape, labels, residue, cleaning buildup, or blocked sound openings.

steps.title: Run the Biomed Audio or Speaker Test
steps.instructions: Use the appropriate Biomed test mode to verify that the primary speaker produces clear, loud, consistent audible alarm or alert tones.

steps.title: Power Cycle and Recheck the Alarm
steps.instructions: Restart the pump after external checks or testing and confirm whether the Primary Audible Alarm POST failure returns.

steps.title: Do Not Return the Pump Until Alarm Function Is Verified
steps.instructions: Return the pump to service only if the POST failure clears, audible alarm output is verified, and normal operational checks are completed.

CCR.Complaint: Medfusion 4000 failed power-on self-test with Primary Audible Alarm POST failure. Audible alarm function could not be verified during startup.
CCR.Cause: Primary audible alarm POST failure was confirmed during startup. Biomed speaker test produced no audible output or intermittent audible output after external power and speaker opening checks were completed.
CCR.Resolution: Removed pump from service and labeled Out of Service. Verified external power, inspected speaker openings for blockage, ran Biomed audio/speaker test, and sent device for repair or bench evaluation due to failed audible alarm verification.

title: Smiths Medical Medfusion 4000 Infusion Pump - Alarm: Pump Motor Drive Off POST
description: Troubleshooting Pump Motor Drive Off POST alarm causing startup failure due to motor drive, drive train, sensing, or control circuit concerns.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove the pump from patient use immediately. Do not use the pump for infusion therapy if it fails POST or reports a motor drive safety alarm. Transfer therapy to another verified pump if needed.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Error/Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power the pump on and confirm the exact alarm displayed is Pump Motor Drive Off POST. Note whether it occurs at startup, during POST, after restart, or after a drop, spill, repair, or battery event.

steps.title: Remove Accessories and Check for Obvious External Issues
steps.instructions: Remove the syringe, tubing, syringe accessories, and any external obstruction around the syringe drive area. Inspect for impact damage, cracked housing, sticky residue, contamination, bent parts, or foreign material in the drive path.

steps.title: Check Power Source and Startup Condition
steps.instructions: Connect the pump to a known-good AC power source and retry startup. Confirm the alarm is not related to low battery, unstable power, or incomplete startup behavior.

steps.title: Inspect the Syringe Drive Area Without Disassembly
steps.instructions: Visually inspect the plunger driver, syringe clamp, flange area, and drive path for jamming, misalignment, damage, contamination, or signs of binding. Do not force the mechanism.

steps.title: Restart Once to Confirm the POST Failure
steps.instructions: Power the pump off, wait briefly, and power it back on while connected to AC power. If the alarm returns, treat it as a device-level safety failure.

steps.title: Enter Biomed Diagnostics, If Available and Allowed
steps.instructions: Use the appropriate Biomed diagnostic mode according to facility procedure. Check motor drive function and related motor, plunger, or rotation verification tests.

steps.title: Evaluate Whether the Issue Is Mechanical or Control-Related
steps.instructions: If diagnostics show abnormal movement or binding, suspect the motor assembly or drive train. If diagnostics show control failure or repeated POST failure without obstruction, suspect the motor drive circuit or Main PCB-related failure.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: If the alarm clears after correcting an external issue, complete full operational testing. Verify startup, motor drive diagnostics, syringe loading, plunger movement, and absence of abnormal noise or binding before returning the pump to service.

CCR.Complaint: Medfusion 4000 infusion pump failed startup with Pump Motor Drive Off POST alarm and could not complete power-on self-test.
CCR.Cause: Pump Motor Drive Off POST alarm confirmed during startup. Syringe setup, external obstruction, visible contamination, damage, and power source issues were checked. Alarm repeated on AC power, indicating likely internal motor drive, drive train, motor sensing, plunger-position sensing, or Main PCB-related fault.
CCR.Resolution: Removed pump from service and labeled Out of Service. Sent for bench evaluation and motor drive diagnostic testing before return to service.
